title: ophrys eleonorae subsp. limensis
intro: Ophrys eleonorae subsp. limensis, also known as the Peruvian bee orchid, is a rare and endemic subspecies of the Ophrys eleonorae orchid native to Peru. It has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The Peruvian bee orchid is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. Herbal preparations of Ophrys eleonorae subsp. limensis are typically made by drying and processing the roots and leaves of the plant, which can be used to make teas, infusions, and other medicinal products.
